steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The student's goal is to explain why brine pools are toxic. The decisive clue is the word “why” — the task requires a cause-and-effect explanation, linking the characteristic that makes brine pools toxic to the actual toxicity.

steps.title: Match the explanation to the choices
steps.content: Choice A directly answers the “why” question by establishing a causal link: the extreme saltiness (the cause) makes the water toxic (the effect). The other three fail to explain causation — B states facts about toxicity without explaining its source, C shifts focus to bacteria survival rather than addressing what causes toxicity, and D describes brine pools without addressing toxicity at all. That matches choice A — A is correct.
